text.title: ITALY: 10 PLACES YOU CAN'T MISS! | Travel Guide
text.u: Italy I feel like everybody at some part
of their life has a desire to go
somewhere in Italy there is just so many
big bucket list items amazing places and
history so I've been to Italy several
times over the last few years and I put
together a list of my top 10 places you
cannot miss if you were going to be
planning a trip to Italy Roma AKA Rome
often regarded as the birth place of
Western Civilization Rome has a crazy
past over 2,500 years since its founding
few cities can offer the amount of art
history and culture that Rome can now I
love Rome it's one of my favorite cities
in Italy because there is just so many
old historic buildings so even though it
is one of the most popular places like
for tourists there's just dozens and
dozens of tourists there it's just you
cannot miss it just so much history
literally the whole Roman Empire is abs
one of my favorite cities in Italy heads
down got to go to Rome also Rome in the
summertime traveling to Rome in the
summer one it's just really hot and two
that's the one like the the most amount
of tourists are there so if you don't
like crowds and heat travel in the off
season to Rome and it's amazing it's
spectacular Turin AKA Torino in the
winter tucked away in the Northwestern
corner of Italy this is a super
underrated City that a lot of tourists
NE so Torino is the Gateway of all of
the winter sports you could possibly
imagine because again they hosted the
Winter Olympic Games so they know a
little bit about winter and things to do
in the winter time so that's when I went
there I got to explore around the city
in winter and it was a really cool place
and I highly recommend the Rona so
Shakespeare set three of his place in
this popular northern city and many
people come here to seek out the famous
house of Juliet and the Bel
that oh Romeo Romeo you know like the
Romeo and Julia balcony it's a famous
thing to do in Verona now one the
coolest things about Verona is that they
have a 2,000-year-old Roman Amphitheater
and they still do annual Fair shows and
operas in the amphitheater which I found
quite spectacular even in comparison to
the Coliseum Venice oh Venice Venice is
located in northeastern Italy and it's
spread across 100 18 small islands in
the venan lagoon so it's also referred
to as the floating city you don't find
any cars here it is all boats and canals
which it is famous for taking a gondola
ride through the various canals of
Venice now unfortunately Venice is a
very in a very peculiar position as it
is a sinking City this is well
documented they're having a lot of
issues with the fact that the city is
built on water and sea levels are rising
so go see Venice go see Venice now while
there is still a Venice not to sound
like depressing or anything but Venice
is changing each and every year go go
see it now and experience the joy of
Venice also be aware though that there
is going to be a lot of tourists there
the main Hub the main Center of Venice
is generally just a tourist City and the
residents live on the outskirts of
Venice which gives it a weird sort of
interesting Vibe but but definitely have
to go check it out ooh the aroma of
lovely Italian wine now if you like
Italian wine you're going to love
Tuscany so Tuscany is a large region in
central Italy that encompasses Rolling
Hills mountains and spectacular types of
art but what it's most famous for is it
collection of internationally renowned
wine region and countless Vineyards
because it's one of the world's best
destinations for wine lovers they
produce a lot of different types of
Italian wines in the various small
cities and Villages that you can find in
the various regions of Tuscany all of
the small villages alone in Tuscany are
just simply stunning there are dozens of
them that you can go visit each having
their own unique charm and character you
can buy tonsen of little Artisan like
pastas and of course lots of wine from
the various vendors and shops you can
visit and Tour all of the wineyards that
you like there are churches on on the
Hills it's just a beautiful beautiful
region of Italy and it's everything that
you imagine Tuscany would be in the
movies that is Tuscany with lots of
lines so uh I will cheers to that Chinko
Terra found on the rugged Coastline of
the Italian Riviera Jinga is a string of
five colorful Villages and one of the
most Charming regions of Italy now much
of its charm comes from the location and
the absence of automobiles and lack of
corporate development one of the most
popular things to do here is actually to
hike the various Trails between the
villages so you can hike up into the
Hillside and hike down to the Villages
and then stay on the villages so it's
like a Ching Tera Trek there's a lot of
different options depending on your
travel abilities and your physical
abilities on how you want to explore the
region chetara is an interesting one
from a Taurus perspective because it's
started as one of the Lesser known
places to visit in Italy when you
compare it to like Venice and Rome but
it actually has a capacity limit so it's
grown so much over the last decade that
they actually limit or at least in some
of the villages try and limit the amount
of tourists that can actually visit them
so if you are planning on visiting
chinatera please do but again it's one
of those like maybe planned for the off
season and you'll have a little bit
better luck do the treack if you can you
know exercise yeah Florence now Florence
is actually the capital of Tuscany and
it has a very colorful and turbulent
history as it is also considered the
birthplace of the Renaissance oh yes
that little movement called the
Renaissance ranked as one of the most
beautiful cities in the world it is
still home to some of the most
recognizable pieces of artistic and
Architectural masterpieces from the
Renaissance literally the most popular
things to do in Florence is to visit the
museums now again museums have a
capacity limit so if you are going to
visit make sure you buy your tickets
beforehand or make sure you line up
early because they have some spectacular
museums there just so much art so much
architecture so much sculptur also
Florence is pretty close to the lean
Tower of Pisa which is still leaning by
the way still a leaning tower of Pisa
the Vatican City while technically it's
not a part of Italy the Vatican City is
a walled Enclave within the city of Rome
it's actually the world's smallest
country but it contains such sites as St
Peter's Basilica the cine Chapel the
museums and some of the world's most
famous paintings and sculptures the
Vatican is just a huge Museum that you
can spend hours and hours browsing
through all the various pieces it's just
such an amazing cool place to visit you
definitely got to visit the Vatican but
you definitely need to book your tickets
online and beforehand so you skip the
long lineups see this is a trend in
Italy Italy is a very popular place so
if you were going to the the Vatican
City I went there in peak season but
just book your tickets online so you
save yourself the huge lineups When in
Rome do the Vatican City cuz it's
literally right there Pompei almost
2,000 years ago Mount theia erupted and
buried the city of Pompei in Ash
perfectly preserving all of the town and
the ruins and even the people yes kind
of creepy definitely creepy a really
cool thing that I personally enjoyed
about pompe was being able to walk
through the streets and feel like you
are walking in an ancient Italian city
because they were still like the streets
were still streets and the buildings the
structure the main structures were still
there like it felt like an ancient city
whereas like in Rome you get buildings
you'll get certain buildings and places
that were preserved all of Pompei was
preserved in my travel experiences it's
one of the most unique things I've ever
been able to experience where I felt
really immersed in what it was like back
then Bergamo nestled in the foothills of
the Alps Berg is about 40 km Northeast
of Milan in the Lombardi region Italy
now the city itself is split into two
parts there's the lower modern part of
the city and then there's the upper
historic part which is connected to by
various cable cars so you could take
cable cars up and you get an amazing
view all over Bergamo it's just a really
good siiz town where you can it's
totally walkable so not like Milan for
example which is just massive burgo is
much more smaller and much more intimate
and you really get that Italian feel of
everything that you're doing minus the
crowds cuz it is not nearly as popular
as all the other places that I've listed
all right and there we have it I hope
youve enjoyed this video and I hope
you've enjoyed learning and discovering
a little bit more about Italy if you've
never been to Italy before if you have
I'd love to know what was your favorite
places to go down below in the comments
and if you haven't been to Italy now
what are your top places of places that
you want to go in Italy like where do
you want to go in Italy let me know down
below and I look forward to reading all
your guys' comments well I hope you have
enjoyed this video if you did please
give it a thumbs up and don't forget to
subscribe to my YouTube channel for More
Travel advice and videos coming your way
every week and I will see you guys again
in a few days time with another video
all right happy travels bye
